---
title: "HYP-0003 GLBX 1-Minute Equity Futures Pairs"
format: html
---

## Status

Initial run completed on 2026-06-19. Status: revise.

## Data

This experiment uses prepared continuous 1-minute root series from
`data/silver/equity_futures_1m_continuous`, built from Databento `GLBX.MDP3`
`ohlcv-1m` outright futures bars.

## Universe

The candidate set is split into major index, micro index, Japan index, and sector
index groups. Pair selection and trading are restricted to pairs within each
group.

## Method

The run compares the existing scalar spread z-score strategy against the
Mahalanobis-distance strategy and a hybrid rule. The hybrid uses z-score for
direction and exits, but only enters or reverses when the rolling Mahalanobis
distance confirms a joint outlier.

## Result

The first run evaluated 73 pairs after minimum-observation filtering and selected
4: `YM-EMD`, `NIY-MNI`, `XAF-XAU`, and `XAP-XAB`.

| Method | Total return | Sharpe | Max drawdown | Active fraction | Turnover | Trades |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Mahalanobis | -1.61% | -0.87 | -4.20% | 41.12% | 346.85 | 630 |
| Z-score | -3.48% | -2.86 | -4.09% | 46.25% | 191.47 | 307 |
| Z-score + Mahalanobis filter | 0.15% | 0.26 | -1.82% | 16.25% | 57.68 | 104 |

Portfolio-level gross/cost decomposition:

| Method | Gross compounded | Cost sum | Net compounded |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Mahalanobis | 3.64% | 5.20% | -1.61% |
| Z-score | -0.67% | 2.87% | -3.48% |
| Z-score + Mahalanobis filter | 1.02% | 0.87% | 0.15% |

For `XAP-XAB`, the hybrid reduced turnover sharply but filtered out too much of
the profitable short-window move:

| Method | Gross return | Cost sum | Net return | Trades |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Mahalanobis | 7.92% | 0.91% | 6.94% | 69 |
| Z-score | 3.64% | 0.32% | 3.30% | 28 |
| Z-score + Mahalanobis filter | 0.04% | 0.10% | -0.06% | 7 |

The selected-pair count is too small for a durable trading claim.

## Decision

Revise. The next iteration should add contract-specific cost assumptions and test
whether `XAP-XAB` survives alternative date splits and Mahalanobis filter
thresholds.
